title: Added `_install_streaming_decode()` function** (lines 226-309)
summary: Added `_install_streaming_decode()` function** (lines 226-309) - Monkey-patches the compression model's decode method - Automatically chunks large token sequences (>30s) during decode - Uses 30s chunks by default (configurable) - Moves each decoded chunk to CPU immediately to free VRAM - Clears CUDA cache after each chunk - Falls back to CPU decode if still OOM with chunking - Concatenates all chunks and returns on original device

title: Added CLI argument** (lines 688-694)
summary: Added CLI argument** (lines 688-694) - `--decode-chunk-duration` (default: 30.0) - Allows users to tune chunk size for VRAM constraints

title: Decode Chunk Duration** (`--decode-chunk-duration` / new "DECODE CHK S" field)
summary: Decode Chunk Duration** (`--decode-chunk-duration` / new "DECODE CHK S" field) - This is how big each **decode chunk** is (default 30 seconds) - This does NOT change the total generation length - It only chunks the **final token â†’ waveform decode step** to avoid OOM

title: You're right
summary: You're right - it probably shouldn't be exposed in the GUI for most users! The 30s default should work for everyone.

title: Generate 30s chunk 2
summary: Generate 30s chunk 2. Stride forward by `extend_stride` (default **18 seconds**) 3. Use the trailing 12s (30-18) as context/prompt for the next 30s chunk 4. Repeat until target duration is reached

title: This is the parameter you probably want to expose:
summary: This is the parameter you probably want to expose: - **Default: 18 seconds** â€” means 12s of context overlap between internal windows - **Lower stride** (e.g., 10) = more context overlap = more sonic continuity, but slower - **Higher stride** (e.g., 25) = less overlap = faster, but more drift/less coherence - **Must be < 30** (assertion enforced)

title: All Generation Parameters (with defaults)

title: Parameter | Default | What It Does | |---|---|---| | `duration` | 30.0 | Target seconds (you'...
summary: | Parameter | Default | What It Does | |---|---|---| | `duration` | 30.0 | Target seconds (you're passing full track duration now) | | `temperature` | 1.0 | Randomness. <1 = deterministic, >1 = wild | | `top_k` | 250 | Top-k sampling (ignored when top_p > 0) | | `top_p` | 0.0 | Nucleus sampling. **Your script uses this instead of top_k** | | `cfg_coef` | 3.0 | Classifier-Free Guidance strength | | `two_step_cfg` | False | Two-pass CFG (slightly different quality, slower) | | `extend_stride` | 18 | Seconds to stride between internal 30s windows | | `use_sampling` | True | Sampling vs greedy argmax |

title: Your `remix()` function trims the input audio to `max_prompt_seconds` (default 3.0s) before e...
summary: Your `remix()` function trims the input audio to `max_prompt_seconds` (default 3.0s) before encoding it as discrete tokens. This becomes the initial prompt for `generate_continuation()`. The model then generates forward from those prompt tokens.

title: This means**: for your full-track generations, `extend_stride` is active and matters
summary: This means**: for your full-track generations, `extend_stride` is active and matters. Lower values (10-14) will give more sonic continuity between windows at the cost of speed. The default 18 leaves only 12s of overlap context, which might explain why long generations can drift stylistically.

title: The sampling step (top_k, top_p, temperature) is a trivial operation â€” sort ~2048 numbers,...
summary: The sampling step (top_k, top_p, temperature) is a trivial operation â€” sort ~2048 numbers, apply a threshold, roll a die. It takes microseconds. The expensive part is the transformer forward pass that produces those 2048 probabilities in the first place, and that runs identically regardless of which sampling method you use afterward.

title: Fair point on all counts
summary: Fair point on all counts. The style suffixes were there because the earlier plan said high-cfg presets could append a style hint â€” but you're right, there's no reason to cut your full prompt short or append random genre keywords. MusicGen's T5 text encoder has a 512-token limit, and your prompt is well within that (~280 words). The full prompt should go in as-is with no suffix.

title: The short suffixes weren't about "definitive results" â€” they were a leftover from when I th...

summary: That's the last one â€” preset 2's successful retry. All background task notifications are now caught up. All 10 presets complete.

title: The intent: strongest possible "follow your training priors, not the input" preset
summary: The intent: strongest possible "follow your training priors, not the input" preset. Near-greedy sampling + below-unity cfg should produce the model's most confident, default-sounding output while the 5s seed gives it slightly more timbral info than 06.

title: lesson**: cfg=0.5 + temp=0.5 = double suppression
summary: 07 lesson**: cfg=0.5 + temp=0.5 = double suppression. The sub-unity cfg actively dampened the audio signal, and near-greedy sampling killed any remaining variance. So it produced something even more lifeless than 06 â€” the model's most "default" output with nothing driving it. The interesting bass appearing at bar 43 is probably the model finally escaping the dead zone of its initial conditioning, but too little too late.

title: You're right that some later presets should behave differently on the seed issue â€” preset 8...
summary: You're right that some later presets should behave differently on the seed issue â€” preset 8 in particular only uses 1s of prompt per chunk (chunked mode), and preset 10 used 0.5s. The chunked ones also re-read corresponding sections of input per chunk rather than just seed-and-forget, so they have a fundamentally different relationship with the input audio.

title: Chunked no-copy**: You're right, if it copied audio it would copy 1s at every chunk start
summary: - **Chunked no-copy**: You're right, if it copied audio it would copy 1s at every chunk start. The reason there's no audible copy is likely that 1s is just too short to perceive as "the same audio" â€” it's below the threshold of recognition, not a mode difference. - **"Prompt"**: Noted â€” I'll say "audio conditioning" or "audio input" to avoid confusion with text prompt. - **Tempo lock**: No built-in MusicGen option for BPM lock. But adding BPM to the text prompt is the right move. Noted. - **Chunk boundaries**: Agreed, the chunks themselves were fine. The drift was the problem, not the chunking.

title: seconds** of audio conditioning from the udio track
summary: - **10 seconds** of audio conditioning from the udio track - **Continuous mode**, stride=18 (default) â€” 12s carryover between windows. Middle ground. - **temp=1.0, top_k=250, cfg=3.0** â€” all MusicGen defaults - **No text prompt

title: The intent: pure baseline
summary: The intent: pure baseline. "What does MusicGen do with 10s of input and completely default settings?" Everything at factory values except the conditioning duration. This is the control experiment â€” whatever this produces is MusicGen's natural behavior with moderate input.

title: You're right, I stand corrected
summary: You're right, I stand corrected. The chunked mode genuinely doesn't copy audio into the output, even at 1s. Continuous mode does. That's a real behavioral difference between the two modes, not a perceptual threshold thing.

title: Should have been 2 bars, 4 bars, 8 bars, 16 bars â€” actual musical units
summary: Should have been 2 bars, 4 bars, 8 bars, 16 bars â€” actual musical units. That's a fundamental design error in the presets. Noted for round 2.

title: The usable bits you described (bars 9-17, bars 26.5-45) sound like they'd be solid material t...
summary: The usable bits you described (bars 9-17, bars 26.5-45) sound like they'd be solid material to cut and rearrange manually â€” which is ironically closer to how ArrangeMe should work: source interesting material, then arrange it with structure.

title: Preset | Audio conditioning | Result | Usability | |--------|------------|--------|----------...
summary: | Preset | Audio conditioning | Result | Usability | |--------|------------|--------|-----------| | 06 | 2s, no guidance | Monotonous kick-hat collapse | Worst | | 07 | 5s, actively dampened | Even more lifeless | Worst | | 08 | 1s chunked, text prompt | Interesting but BPM drift | Material source, drift kills it | | 09 | 10s, defaults | Chaotic but genuinely creative | Best â€” cut and rearrange | | 10 | 0.5s, high temp | Decent start â†’ textured noise | First 15 bars usable |

title: You're right â€” we agreed the full Bunker/Viewlexx prompt would be included in every preset,...
summary: You're right â€” we agreed the full Bunker/Viewlexx prompt would be included in every preset, with me optionally adding emphasis or experiment-specific additions on top. Instead I only included it in preset 8 and left the rest with no text prompt at all. That's a major oversight â€” the text prompt is the main tool for steering the model away from its generic metallic MusicGen defaults, and I left it out of 4 out of 5 udio presets.

title: kHz sample rate** â€” MusicGen's EnCodec codec operates at 32kHz natively
summary: - **32kHz sample rate** â€” MusicGen's EnCodec codec operates at 32kHz natively. The small model outputs 32kHz regardless. It should be resampled to 44.1kHz or 48kHz on output. - **Mono** â€” MusicGen small is a mono-only model. There's a separate `facebook/musicgen-stereo-small` model but we didn't use it. - **Stereo from one speaker** â€” likely the WAV has a stereo header but only left channel has data (mono signal in a stereo container).

title: For round 2 we should: 1
summary: For round 2 we should: 1. Use `musicgen-stereo-small` instead (same VRAM, actual stereo output) 2. Resample to 44.1kHz on output via ffmpeg 3. If stereo model doesn't work on 8GB, at minimum duplicate mono to both channels

title: e
